subject: Biology
title: Fermentation: Life Without Oxygen
description: A fast breakdown of Fermentation: Life Without Oxygen for builders.

hook.text: When oxygen runs out, cells don't just die. Some switch to a backup plan.

buildup.text: Fermentation extracts energy from glucose without oxygen, but far less efficiently.

discovery.text: Yeast ferments sugar into ethanol and CO₂. That's how we get bread, beer, and wine.

twist.text: Your muscles ferment glucose into lactic acid during hard exercise—that's the burn.

climax.text: Fermentation kept life going for billions of years before oxygen filled the atmosphere.

punchline.text: Life's backup generator still runs today.

quiz.question: What does yeast produce during fermentation?
